title: descurainia appendiculata
intro: Descurainia appendiculata, also known as western wallflower or tumbleweed mustard, is a plant species in the mustard family that is native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Descurainia appendiculata include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Descurainia appendiculata can be prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, and it is also sometimes used in herbal supplements and salves.
